title: Define calculated ID formula/source pattern
target: Per-table calculated/display ID candidates
done_criteria: For each candidate table, define source fields, formula/display pattern, expected output examples, blank handling, and whether the formula supplements or eventually replaces a manual ID field.
validation_notes: Approval packet must include formula text or helper-field logic, candidate table/field ids, examples from real records, and external-reference risk.
context: Calculated IDs are for searchability and consistency; they must not break existing stable references.

title: Stage calculated ID field approval packet
target: Schema create/update payload for calculated ID helpers
done_criteria: Packet lists exact target tables, field names, field types, formula/source logic, examples, dependency notes, and rollback plan before any schema write.
validation_notes: Pause for explicit approval before creating fields.
context: No direct conversion or deletion of legacy ID fields in this task.

title: Verify calculated ID readback
target: After-readback for calculated ID fields
done_criteria: Read back created fields and sample records; verify formulas/helper values produce expected IDs and do not create blanks or collisions.
validation_notes: Completion requires after_readback_verification artifact and observed sample outputs.
context: Do not claim ID work complete from schema-create success alone.

title: Dedupe signature fields and duplicate prevention
target: Per-table dedupe signatures and duplicate candidate workflows
done_criteria: Each table that needs duplicate prevention has a defined signature rule, candidate field design, duplicate report, and merge/delete gate.
validation_notes: No merge, delete, or destructive update occurs in this workstream without separate approval packets.
context: Dedupe signatures should prevent future duplication and support safe candidate discovery.

title: Define per-table dedupe signature rules
target: Tables with repeatable operational objects
done_criteria: Rules identify natural key fields, normalization method, null handling, intentional duplicate exceptions, and whether signature should be formula/text/helper.
validation_notes: Exclude tables where duplicates are valid historical events unless a same-event duplicate rule is explicit.
context: Lifecycle ledgers and evidence logs may allow repeated similar records; authority tables usually need stronger dedupe.

title: Stage dedupe_signature field creation candidates
target: dedupe_signature helper fields
done_criteria: Approval packet lists table, proposed field name/type/formula or backfill source, expected signature examples, and collision policy.
validation_notes: Pause before schema writes. Do not create fields in tables where a signature rule is not approved.
context: Signature fields support dedupe prevention and later duplicate reports.

title: Generate duplicate candidate report
target: Duplicate signatures and duplicate candidate groups
done_criteria: Report lists duplicate groups with table, signature, record ids, primary values, confidence, retain/merge/delete recommendation, and reason.
validation_notes: Report only; no record mutation, merge, Delete Queue row, or deletion.
context: Duplicate candidates are evidence for later approval packets, not automatic cleanup.

title: Stage merge/delete candidates separately
target: Duplicate cleanup candidate packets
done_criteria: For each candidate action, packet identifies canonical record, duplicate record, exact field merge behavior, evidence preservation, target deletion mechanism, and approval requirement.
validation_notes: No Delete Queue rows or destructive edits without explicit approval for the exact packet.
context: Merge decisions are high-impact because evidence and links can be lost.

title: Verify dedupe signature and duplicate-prevention readback
target: After-readback for dedupe fields/reports
done_criteria: Verify created fields exist, signatures populate as expected, duplicate candidate counts match report, and no approved exclusions are flagged incorrectly.
validation_notes: Completion requires after_readback_verification artifact.
context: Dedupe prevention is not complete until both field/readback and candidate-report accuracy are checked.

title: Computed and operational helper fields
target: search_text, normalized_key, is_current, needs_review, aging helpers
done_criteria: Design helper fields only where they improve filtering, search, status review, dedupe, or operational routing.
validation_notes: No helper field creation before table-by-table candidate packet is approved.
context: Helper fields should reduce ambiguity and improve views/search, not create clutter.

title: Identify helper-field inclusion criteria
target: Potential computed/helper field families
done_criteria: Criteria distinguish search helper, normalization helper, Boolean review helper, aging helper, and rollup-like helper fields. Exclude helpers that duplicate existing reliable fields.
validation_notes: Criteria must specify why each helper improves filtering/search/operation.
context: Avoid adding helper fields just because formulas are possible.

title: Stage helper-field approval packet
target: Candidate helper fields by table
done_criteria: Packet lists table, field name, field type/formula/source, operational use, expected value examples, and view/filter that will consume it.
validation_notes: Pause before schema writes.
context: Every helper field must have a consuming workflow/view/search use case.

title: Backfill helper fields when formulas are unavailable
target: Non-formula helper fields requiring data backfill
done_criteria: For each non-formula helper, planned_payload and target_records specify exact values, before gates, and expected counts.
validation_notes: Use chatgpt-exec for bulk backfill; verify after-readback.
context: Prefer formulas where possible; use backfill only where Airtable field type or behavior requires stored values.

title: Verify helper-field usefulness and remove bad candidates
target: Helper field after-readback and utility check
done_criteria: Verify values populate correctly and at least one view/filter/search/workflow uses or will use each helper. Bad candidates are marked not implemented, not left as clutter.
validation_notes: Do not claim completion unless utility and readback are both proven.
context: Operational usefulness is a completion criterion.

title: Field deletion and retirement candidates
target: Blank, obsolete, duplicate, replaced, or confusing fields
done_criteria: Field candidates are ranked with evidence and routed through prefix/retire/delete approval gates.
validation_notes: No deletion without exact target/action/payload approval and dependency checks.
context: Aggressive cleanup is desired, but destructive field work is gated.

title: Rank field candidates by evidence
target: High-blank, obsolete, duplicate, replaced, or ambiguous fields
done_criteria: Report includes table, field id/name/type, blank ratio, observed values, replacement field if any, dependency risk, and recommendation.
validation_notes: Known examples to evaluate include blank active_plan_task_id, source_idea_id, source_checkpoint_id, canonical_parent_plan_id; final candidates must come from fresh profile/readback.
context: Blankness alone is not sufficient for deletion if the field is future-facing or externally referenced.

title: Prepare non-destructive field retirement packet
target: Fields to prefix or mark as DELETE candidates
done_criteria: Packet proposes safe prefix/mark action for candidate fields where dependency risk remains. Includes before field name, new field name, reason, and rollback name.
validation_notes: Pause before schema writes. Prefixing is still schema mutation.
context: Prefix/mark is preferred before deletion when dependencies are uncertain.

title: Prepare destructive field deletion packet
target: Fields approved for deletion consideration
done_criteria: Packet lists exact field id/name/table, replacement proof, dependency scan, before snapshot, deletion method, expected after schema, and stop conditions.
validation_notes: Requires explicit approval immediately before deletion attempt. If Airtable API cannot delete field, produce manual/Scripting Extension instructions.
context: Do not combine deletion approval with candidate discovery.

title: Verify field retirement/deletion readback
target: Post-field-retirement schema readback
done_criteria: Verify prefixed/deleted fields match approved packet and no non-target fields changed.
validation_notes: After_readback_verification must include schema diff and target-by-target pass/fail.
context: Schema success must be proven by live schema readback.

title: Table retirement and replacement candidates
target: Cleanup-named scaffold tables, obsolete tables, and replacement-table patterns
done_criteria: Each table disposition has explicit keep/replace/retire rationale, reference update plan, and approval gate.
validation_notes: No table deletion via Delete Queue; table deletion/renaming remains manual or schema-action gated with readback.
context: Delete Queue semantics are preserved and Delete Queue is excluded from broad analysis unless explicitly requested.

title: Decide generic WBS replacement table path
target: DCOIR Cleanup WBS and candidate DCOIR Execution WBS
done_criteria: Decision states whether to create a new generic WBS table, migrate rows, update references, and mark old cleanup-named table for retirement.
validation_notes: This is the explicit answer to the table-renaming/scaffold decision.
context: Operator confirmed table replacement is acceptable when old references can be updated.

title: Inventory references to WBS/scaffold tables
target: Skills, Airtable rows, repo scripts, docs, operator tools, and workflows referencing WBS/scaffold tables
done_criteria: Report lists each reference, source location/table/field/file, whether it must be updated, and proposed new reference.
validation_notes: Use Airtable search/readback and GitHub source search where needed.
context: Replacement is only safe if references are updated or proven historical/harmless.

title: Prepare table replacement migration packet
target: New WBS table creation and row migration
done_criteria: Packet contains exact new table name, fields/options, source rows to migrate, target rows to create, reference updates, and old-table disposition.
validation_notes: Pause before creating replacement table or changing references.
context: This is high-impact schema work even without deletion.

title: Prepare old-table retirement packet
target: Old cleanup/scaffold tables after migration
done_criteria: Packet proposes DELETE-prefix or manual retirement for old table only after migration/reference readback succeeds.
validation_notes: No broad table deletion or Delete Queue usage for table deletion. Preserve evidence and lifecycle notes.
context: Tables and records are governed differently; Delete Queue is for rows, not table deletion.

title: Remaining aging and backfill cleanup
target: created_at, updated_at, review_after, retention/status blanks and aging fields
done_criteria: Backfill candidates are staged with exact before values and evidence; approved writes are verified by after-readback.
validation_notes: No blanket backfill without source-basis or approved default rule.
context: Aging/backfill cleanup should improve reviewability and filtering.

title: Inventory aging/backfill gaps
target: Aging/review/status/retention fields across operational tables
done_criteria: Report lists blanks, invalid dates, stale review_after, retention/status blanks, and proposed source/default for each backfill class.
validation_notes: Include exclusion reasons where backfill should not occur.
context: The previous cleanup reduced many gaps; this task handles remaining evidence-backed gaps.

title: Stage aging/backfill payload with before-value gates
target: Approved remaining backfill candidates
done_criteria: planned_payload lists record id, field, before value, after value, source basis, and expected count for each update.
validation_notes: Use chatgpt-exec for bulk updates and stop on before-value mismatch.
context: Backfill must be deterministic and auditable.

title: Verify aging/backfill after-readback
target: After-readback for backfilled fields
done_criteria: Verify expected records changed, no unexpected fields changed, and remaining blanks match approved exclusions.
validation_notes: Completion requires execution_summary and after_readback_verification.
context: Do not claim cleanup from write success alone.
